w:r.w:t: Needs Assessment → Concept development → Approval → Programme/course Development/Review → Approval by Academic Board → Verification and Validation by QAD → Submission to Mak/NCHE
Interpretation of the components in the SDSs matrix
Defines the specific service being delivered by MUBS. It states what the institution is expected to provide as a service (e.g., admissions, registration, timetabling, training, and graduation).

Indicates how performance will be measured and how the office responsible is expected to report. These metrics show whether the service meets the required standards, such as the number of students registered or the timeliness of timetables.
Standard (Quality, Process Time, Accessibility, Coverage, Frequency)
Defines the minimum acceptable level of service. It outlines:
– the step-by-step sequence of activities required to deliver the service (e.g., registration → invoice → payment → receipt)
– how users access the information about the service.
– who is included or targeted by the service.
– how often the service is delivered
Identifies the primary users or recipients of the service (e.g., students, staff, applicants, teachers). It answers who the service is meant for.
Lists the requirements or conditions a user must meet to receive the service (e.g., paid fees, a valid ID, and an admission letter). Ensures fairness and transparency.
Describes the step-by-step process through which the service is delivered. It outlines how the service is executed from start to finish.
Specifies the resources needed to deliver the service effectively (e.g., ICT systems, staff, policies, equipment, venues).
Identifies the unit or office accountable for delivering the service (e.g., Office of the School Registrar, QAD, HODs). Ensures clear accountability.
Needs Assessment → Concept development → Approval → Programme/course Development/Review → Approval by Academic Board → Verification and Validation by QAD → Submission to Mak/NCHE
Standard (Quality, Time, Accessibility, Coverage and Frequency)
No. of programmes/course developed within approved timelines.
% of programmes/course reviewed within approved timelines.
No. of trainings conducted on curriculum/course development and review (CBET).
Adherence to Approved NCHE, Mak, and MUBS QA Policy and Guidelines.

Needs Assessment (2 months) → Concept development (1 month) → Approval (1 month) → Programme/courses Development/Review (4 months) → Approval by Academic Board (1 month) → Verification and Validation by QAD (1 month) → Submission to Mak/NCHE (2 months)
Development as per schedule; Review as per NCHE guidelines
